description:
The skill's "Scripting with bl" section says bl prime/ready/list/show all accept --json and print "a single JSON document to stdout", but does not document what *shape* each one returns. Shapes are inconsistent:

- bl prime --json  → object: {identity, claimed, claimed_status, ready}
- bl ready --json  → bare array
- bl list  --json  → bare array
- bl show ID --json → object (per-task)

This caused at least one agent to write `data['ready']` against `bl ready --json` output (a bare list), having seen the `ready` key inside `bl prime --json`. TypeError, traceback, dead loop iteration.

Fix: add a small table or per-command lines to the "Scripting with bl" section in src/skill.md showing top-level JSON shape (object with which keys, vs. bare array of task objects) for each --json command. Should be terse — a couple of lines per command, in line with the existing "single token on success" table.

Related but separate: bl-f0b8 fixes the prime --json stdout leak. This task is the doc-only side and is independently useful even after that fix lands.
title: skill: document JSON shape per --json command
